tools.name: get_analysis_status
tools.description:
Check the processing status of an uploaded paper.

    Poll this tool after uploading a PDF until status is 'Ready' before
    calling get_variable_relationships.

    Args:
        file_id: The file_id returned by the /upload endpoint.
        authorization: Optional. API key as 'Bearer hk_...' or

tools.name: get_variable_relationships
tools.description:
Retrieve analyzed variable relationships for a completed paper.

    Only returns results when get_analysis_status reports status='Ready'.

    Without an API key (anonymous): returns the relationship list with
    source, target, and direction — but detailed reasoning is available
    only with an

tools.name: initiate_device_auth
tools.description:
Start a browser-based sign-in flow to get an API key for full access.

    Call this when you need detailed analysis results (reasoning, measurements)
    that require authentication. Returns a verification URL to show to the user.
    After the user signs in, poll check_device_auth with the returne

tools.name: check_device_auth
tools.description:
Check if the user has completed browser sign-in for a device auth request.

    Poll this after calling initiate_device_auth. Returns status 'pending'
    while waiting, or 'complete' with an api_key when the user has signed in.
    Use the returned api_key as the authorization parameter in other to

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_analysis_status" references sensitive artifacts (authorization, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: initiate_device_auth · Private data: get_analysis_status, get_variable_relationships, initiate_device_auth · Public sink: get_analysis_status, get_variable_relationships.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: initiate_device_auth · Private data: get_analysis_status, get_variable_relationships, initiate_device_auth · Public sink: get_analysis_status, get_variable_relationships

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
